guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.name: HK → Brazil — USD-referenced stablecoin B2B settlement
data.feasibility_class: Category II (directed edge: channelled — counterparty-conditional + size-capped; the undirected HK-BR pair is I/II)
data.flow: A USD-referenced stablecoin used as the settlement medium for a B2B cross-border flow, originating on the Hong Kong side and terminating on the Brazil side. Ticket size is the hinge: both Brazil crypto rails are capped per operation, so corridor feasibility differs sharply between sub-cap and above-cap tickets.

sub_gates.label: Issuer side — Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656)
sub_gates.requirement: A licence is required only if the stablecoin is (i) issued in Hong Kong, or (ii) referencing the HKD (in or outside HK), or (iii) actively marketed to the HK public. A USD-referenced coin is NOT HKD-referencing, so limb (ii) does not apply. Limb (i) "issued in HK" is currency-agnostic and turns on the HKMA factor test: place of incorporation; day-to-day management; minting/burning location; where reserve assets are managed; where the mint/redeem bank account is held.
sub_gates.analysis: The regime permits a USD-referenced FRS licence in principle (limb (i) is currency-agnostic), BUT no USD FRS has been licensed: the first cohort (10 Apr 2026) licensed only HKD-referenced issuers — Anchorpoint (FRS01, HKDAP = "HKD At Par") and HSBC (FRS02) — and HKMA commentary signals no near-term USD-pegged path. So for this corridor the live HK route for a USD coin is NOT a domestic FRS licence; it is an offshore-issued non-HKD FRS (e.g. a US issuer) that is not "issued in HK" on the factor test and not actively marketed to HK retail. (Note: HKDAP tracks USD only indirectly — the HKD is pegged to USD at 7.75–7.85 under the Linked Exchange Rate System — but it is legally an HKD instrument and is a different instrument from a USD coin.)

sub_gates.label: Distribution side — permitted offerors
sub_gates.requirement: Offering an FRS does not itself need a stablecoin licence, but only "permitted offerors" may offer an FRS in HK — even one issued offshore / non-HKD: the licensed FRS issuer, authorised institutions (banks), SFC-licensed VATPs, SFC Type 1 corporations, and SVF licensees. An FRS from an issuer not HK-licensed (e.g. a foreign non-HKD FRS) may be offered to professional investors only.
sub_gates.analysis: With no domestic USD FRS available, channel is the operative HK constraint: distribution of the USD coin must run through a permitted offeror, and (because the issuer is not HK-licensed) to professional investors only. The PI-only limb is not binding for B2B — institutional counterparties typically qualify as PIs.

sub_gates.label: Settlement into tokenised funds — SFC securities perimeter
sub_gates.requirement: Where the flow routes a stablecoin balance into a tokenised money-market fund, that fund is a "tokenised security" under the SFO (an interest in a CIS). The SFC's Tokenised Securities Circular (2 Nov 2023, 23EC52/23EC53) applies "same business, same risks, same rules" on a see-through basis; dealing/ distribution needs the relevant SFC licence (Type 1, etc.). Absent SFC authorisation under Part IV SFO / prospectus compliance, the offer is PI-only.
sub_gates.analysis: HK analogue of the US Reves/§404 securities-classification spine: in HK a tokenised MMF is squarely a tokenised security, with SFC-authorised retail instances already live (retail tokenised MMFs authorised Q1 2025). HK gives a clean "yes, it is a security, and here is the regulated path".
sub_gates.gap_note: No HK securities_classification record exists yet — this sub-gate is the spec for it.

boundary_analysis.clears: A B2B USD-stablecoin settlement clears the HK side where: the coin is an offshore non-HKD FRS not actively marketed to HK retail (a domestically-licensed USD FRS does not yet exist — first cohort is HKD-only); it is offered only via a permitted offeror to professional investors (B2B counterparties qualify); and any tokenised- fund leg uses an SFC-authorised tokenised product or is confined to PIs under the tokenised-securities-circular controls.
boundary_analysis.breaks: Issuing a USD-referenced stablecoin in HK (per the factor test) without an HKMA FRS licence; actively marketing the coin to the HK retail public; offering through a non-permitted offeror; or routing into a tokenised MMF/security and distributing it to HK retail without SFC authorisation / prospectus compliance (PI-only otherwise).
sources: Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656), s.14(1) licensing; Schedule 2 minimum criteria
HKMA Explanatory Note on Licensing of Stablecoin Issuers (29 Jul 2025) — 'issued in HK' factor test; permitted offerors; non-HKD FRS PI-only
SFC Circulars on tokenised securities-related activities & tokenised SFC-authorised products (2 Nov 2023, 23EC52 / 23EC53)
SFC circular permitting secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised products (20 Apr 2026)
Live cohort: HKMA first FRS licences 10 Apr 2026 — Anchorpoint (FRS01, HKDAP/HKD-referenced) + HSBC (FRS02, HKD-referenced); NO USD FRS licensed
Live instances: SFC-authorised retail tokenised MMFs (Q1 2025)

sub_gates.label: eFX rail — closing to crypto on the external leg
sub_gates.requirement: Res 561 (pub. 30 Apr 2026; in force 1 Oct 2026; amends Res 277/2022, which regulates the FX Law, Lei 14.286/2021) bars eFX providers from settling the external leg — provider ↔ foreign counterparty (contraparte no exterior) — with virtual assets; settlement must be via an FX operation or a non-resident BRL (reais) account. eFX is the mass/low-value retail rail: per-operation ceilings of USD 10k (unilateral transfers; same-owner transfers; and, new under 561, financial/capital-market investment transfers); it is not a high-value B2B rail.
sub_gates.analysis: The ban is specific to the eFX settlement leg, not a general stablecoin ban — crypto trading, custody, and other arrangements remain permitted under the VASP regime. The USD 10k eFX ceiling independently confirms eFX was never the natural B2B rail; the practical effect is to foreclose the "reais → USDT → settle abroad on-chain" eFX back-end (the Nomad/Braza-Bank-style rail).

sub_gates.label: VASP-in-FX rail — open but capped
sub_gates.requirement: Res 521 (pub. 10 Nov 2025; in force 2 Feb 2026; amends the FX-law trio Res 277/278/279 that regulate Lei 14.286/2021 — inserting Art. 76-A into Res 277 as the "ativos virtuais no mercado de câmbio" hook) brings authorised VASPs' (SPSAVs') virtual-asset international payments/transfers into the FX market as legitimate câmbio operations, subject to a per-operation cap that bites WHEN THE FOREIGN COUNTERPARTY IS NOT a BCB-FX-authorised institution: USD 100k for a SPSAV; USD 500k for an FX-authorised institution (bank / DTVM / CTVM / corretora de câmbio). Audit + monthly FX reporting to the BCB from 4 May 2026.
sub_gates.analysis: For a USD-stablecoin B2B settlement the foreign counterparty is typically not BCB-FX-authorised, so the cap bites. The cap — not the rail choice — is usually the binding constraint for B2B ticket sizes; above it the flow reverts to traditional câmbio (bank FX desk / DTVM).

sub_gates.label: Custody, segregation & asset curation — Res 520
sub_gates.requirement: Res 520 (in force 2 Feb 2026) requires a documented patrimonial-segregation policy (Art. 28–30): client fiat in individualised accounts; client virtual assets in wallets distinct from the provider's own-operation wallets; the methods used for proof of reserves; and an independent audit of the provider's and clients' virtual assets on a BIENNIAL basis at reasonable-assurance level (Art. 30 III), with the report publicly disclosed on the provider's website (Art. 30 §1). Up to 5% own-asset co-mingling is allowed for immediate liquidity if identified/recorded. Asset curation (Art. 64+) PROHIBITS offering/intermediating algorithmic stablecoins or any fiat-pegged token without effective, verifiable liquid-asset backing; the "ativo de reserva" is defined as fiat + government bonds issued by the same government. Foreign custodians may be used only under home- country authorisation/supervision, with a Brazil representative, enforceable guarantees, formal segregation of Brazilian clients' assets, and record access.
sub_gates.analysis: A USD stablecoin used in this corridor must be an auditable-reserve coin; an algorithmic or opaque-reserve coin is excluded at the Brazilian custody/listing gate regardless of the rail. The foreign-custodian conditions are directly relevant to the HK/offshore custody leg of this corridor.

boundary_analysis.clears: Authorised-VASP-in-FX channel (Res 521), within the per-operation cap (USD 100k SPSAV / USD 500k FX-authorised institution where the foreign counterparty is not BCB-FX-authorised), with audit + monthly FX reporting from 4 May 2026 and Res 520 custody/segregation (Art. 28–30: proof-of-reserves, biennial reasonable-assurance audit, public report), using an auditable-reserve (non-algorithmic) stablecoin.
boundary_analysis.breaks: eFX back-end stablecoin settlement rail (Res 561) — closing 1 Oct 2026. Tickets above the SPSAV / FI per-operation caps (no crypto rail available — reverts to traditional câmbio). Algorithmic or non-auditable-reserve stablecoins (excluded by Res 520 asset-curation rules, Art. 64+).
sources: BCB Resolutions 519/2025, 520/2025, 521/2025 (pub. 10 Nov 2025; in force 2 Feb 2026; audit/reporting from 4 May 2026)
BCB Resolution 561/2026 (pub. 30 Apr 2026; in force 1 Oct 2026; amends Res 277/2022)
Res 520 Art. 28–30 (segregation; proof-of-reserves methods; biennial reasonable-assurance audit; public website disclosure); Art. 64+ (asset curation / algorithmic-stablecoin prohibition)
Lei 14.286/2021 (Foreign Exchange Law) — the FX statute regulated by Res 277/278/279 and amended by Res 521/561 (legal authority: arts. 2-6, 10, 14, 15, 18, per the Res 521 recital)
Res 277/2022 Art. 76-A (inserted by Res 521; new Título VIII-A) — the 'prestação de serviços de ativos virtuais incluída no mercado de câmbio' hook
Lei 14.478/2022 (Marco Legal dos Ativos Virtuais); Decreto 11.563/2023 (designates BCB; preserves CVM jurisdiction over securities-like tokens)
Instrução Normativa BCB 701 (22 Jan 2026) — independent technical certification for intermediation/custody

data.key_constraint: The binding constraint is regulatory geography COMPOUNDED BY A SIZING CEILING, and the two legs gate on different axes. Brazil offers no single uniform crypto rail: the eFX mass-payment rail's external settlement leg is closing to crypto (Res 561, 1 Oct 2026) and is in any case capped at USD 10k/op, so a stablecoin B2B flow must use the authorised- VASP-in-FX channel (Res 521) — itself capped per operation (USD 100k SPSAV / USD 500k FX-authorised institution, where the foreign counterparty is not BCB-FX-authorised) and limited to auditable-reserve, non-algorithmic stablecoins (Res 520). Above those caps, neither crypto rail is available and the flow reverts to traditional câmbio. Hong Kong gates the SAME instrument on a different axis — not a numeric cap but a perimeter/ eligibility line: issued-in-HK FRS licensing, permitted-offeror distribution, professional-investor-only for non-HKD FRS (and, today, the absence of any licensed USD FRS — first cohort is HKD-only), and (if the flow touches a tokenised fund) the SFC securities perimeter. A single USD-stablecoin instrument therefore cannot serve both legs as one uniform rail: HK gates on WHO may issue/offer and TO WHOM; Brazil gates on WHICH rail and UP TO WHAT SIZE. Four-way composition (rail × cap × auditable-reserve × HK eligibility), not a single prohibition.
data.us_doctrinal_link: Both legs turn on the same question as US §404's bona-fide-activity carve-out: what activity is permitted on the regulated stablecoin rail. Brazil answers by rail-and-cap (eFX closed; VASP-in-FX open but capped), with a parallel securities axis: the BCB/CVM jurisdictional split (Decreto 11.563/2023 preserves CVM authority over securities-like tokens) is the Brazilian analogue of the US Reves/§404 securities-classification spine — though Brazil's C4 contribution is currently token-classification + asset-curation (Res 520), not yet a developed routing-into-tokenised-fund story (the CVM-175 fund analogue would be the true parallel to the US 1940-Act MMF routing question). HK answers by perimeter-and-eligibility and treats a tokenised MMF as a tokenised security. US remains the anchor instance; HK and Brazil are two contrasting resolutions of the identical boundary.

watch_list.event: BR VASP non-authorised-provider prohibition (Res 519/520) + 270-day grandfathering ends
eFX-modality Unicad registration deadline for already-authorised institutions (Res 561 Art 56-A)
Unauthorised eFX providers must apply for payment-institution authorisation (Res 561 Art 56-B)
IOF on stablecoin/FX ops — fato gerador created by Res 521; NO Receita Federal norm in force as of Jun 2026 (proposed IOF-Câmbio 3.5% vs 'private FX' 1.1% — in public consultation, no date)
BR Travel Rule phase-in stage 1 — domestic PSAV-to-PSAV transfers (Res 520 Art. 89)
BR Travel Rule full compliance — international transfers + carteiras autocustodiadas (Res 520 Art. 89)
SFC tokenisation perimeter — secondary-trading circular bedding in
HKMA second-cohort posture on non-HKD / USD-referenced pegs

data.phase_in_note: Brazil is in rolling implementation: Res 561 in force 1 Oct 2026; non-authorised-provider prohibition and eFX-modality Unicad registration 30 Oct 2026; unauthorised eFX providers must seek payment-institution authorisation by 31 May 2027; Travel Rule fully phased by 2 Feb 2028; IOF treatment unresolved. A reader after mid-2026 should re-verify these moving parts before relying on the corridor.
data.reviewed_against: Firm/practitioner analysis of BCB Res 519/520/521 + 561 (Notabene, Cescon, BDO, Global Legal Insights, Conduit) and the official BCB normativo URLs (confirmed to exist); the article-level pinpoints (e.g. Res 520 Art 29/30, Res 561 ementa amending Res 277/2022) are taken from those firm summaries and NOT yet read against the official DOU/BCB text. Statutory base: Lei 14.286/2021 (FX) + Lei 14.478/2022 + Decreto 11.563/2023; the câmbio-inclusion hook Art. 76-A is a provision inserted into Res 277/2022 by Res 521 (confirmed against the Res 521 DOU text, Jun 2026 — not, as some firm summaries imply, an article of Lei 14.286/2021). HK side: Cap.656 + HKMA Explanatory Note + SFC 23EC52/23EC53; the HKDAP=HKD-referenced fact is confirmed from the Standard Chartered/Anchorpoint press release (Tier 1), HSBC HKD-referenced.

infrastructure_overlap.note: HK on Project Ensemble + mBridge; BR on no shared wholesale-settlement experiment. At least one end off the shared experiments.

verification_backlog.instrument: SFC circular permitting secondary trading of tokenised SFC-authorised products (20 Apr 2026)

class_basis.instrument: BCB VASP authorization + the Resolution 521 counterparty-conditional câmbio channel (licensed VASP only).
